source_paper_title: Inexact Bregman Proximal Gradient Method and Its Inertial Variant with Absolute and Partial Relative Stopping Criteria
context:
Section 3 (discussion after Algorithm 1), page 9

Although this requirement can be satisfied in many cases (see two examples in the following subsection), it may limit the applicability of (ReSC). Note that Bello-Cruz et al. [18] recently incorporated a full relative-type error rule in the FISTA, which can accommodate a nonzero error \(\Delta_k\). Thus, it would be interesting to explore whether the requirement \(\|\Delta_k\| = 0\) can be removed in our methods, which we will leave for future research.
title: Allow nonzero optimality-residual error \(\Delta_k\) under the relative stopping criterion in inexact Bregman proximal gradient methods
latex:
Given a real finite-dimensional Euclidean space \(E\) with inner product \(\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle\), a closed convex set \(Q\subseteq E\) with nonempty interior, and proper closed convex functions \(P,f:E\to(-\infty,+\infty]\) such that \(f\) is differentiable on \(\mathrm{int}\,Q\), consider the convex composite minimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in Q}\; F(x):=P(x)+f(x).
\]
Let \(\phi:E\to(-\infty,+\infty]\) be an essentially smooth, strictly convex kernel with \(\mathrm{dom}\,\phi=Q\), and define the Bregman distance
\[
D_\phi(u,v):=\phi(u)-\phi(v)-\langle\nabla\phi(v),u-v\rangle,
\]
for \(u\in \mathrm{dom}\,\phi\) and \(v\in \mathrm{int}\,\mathrm{dom}\,\phi\). Fix parameters \(\lambda>L\) and \(\sigma\in[0,1)\), where \(L\ge 0\) is such that \(f\) is \(L\)-smooth relative to \(\phi\) (possibly restricted to a closed convex set \(X\supseteq \mathrm{dom}\,P\cap\mathrm{dom}\,\phi\)).

At iteration \(k\ge 0\), define the Bregman proximal-gradient subproblem
\[
\min_{x\in Q}\; P(x)+\langle\nabla f(x_k),x-x_k\rangle+\lambda D_\phi(x,x_k).
\]
An inexact method produces \(x_{k+1}\in X\cap \mathrm{int}\,\mathrm{dom}\,\phi\), \(\tilde x_{k+1}\in \mathrm{dom}\,P\cap\mathrm{dom}\,\phi\), and error terms \(\Delta_k\in E\), \(\delta_k\ge 0\) satisfying the perturbed optimality condition
\[
\Delta_k\in \partial_{\delta_k}P(\tilde x_{k+1})+\nabla f(x_k)+\lambda\bigl(\nabla\phi(x_{k+1})-\nabla\phi(x_k)\bigr),
\]
where \(\partial_{\delta}P(y):=\{g\in E: P(z)\ge P(y)+\langle g,z-y\rangle-\delta\ \forall z\in E\}\).

The paper’s relative stopping criterion (ReSC) requires \(\|\Delta_k\|=0\) and
\[
\lambda^{-1}\delta_k + D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_{k+1}) \le \sigma\, D_\phi(\tilde x_{k+1},x_k).
\]
The problem is to design and analyze a relative-type stopping criterion that allows \(\|\Delta_k\|\) to be nonzero while still guaranteeing convergence (e.g., \(F(\tilde x_k)\to \inf_{x\in Q}F(x)\)) and, ideally, an \(O(1/k)\) function-value convergence rate (at least for averaged iterates or last iterates), under verifiable assumptions and explicit conditions relating \(\Delta_k\), \(\delta_k\), and the Bregman distances.
open_problem_quote: Although this requirement can be satisfied in many cases (see two examples in the following subsection), it may limit the applicability of (ReSC). Note that Bello-Cruz et al. [18] recently incorporated a full relative-type error rule in the FISTA, which can accommodate a nonzero error \(\Delta_k\). Thus, it would be interesting to explore whether the requirement \(\|\Delta_k\| = 0\) can be removed in our methods, which we will leave for future research.
